region_of_origin: Middle East (Levant); served as a Western-style fast-food meal in Singapore
food_subgroup: Legume-based fried snack meal with side and beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a falafel food product entry; the full meal set (5 nuggets + side + drink) is not individually coded. closest_parent applied to the falafel component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers falafel as a food item; the composite meal set is not individually coded. closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for falafel nuggets meal; none assigned.

serving.description: 1 meal set (5 falafel nuggets + side + beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal set consisting of 5 falafel nuggets (approximately 150 g), a side portion such as a bread roll or salad (approximately 150 g), and a beverage (approximately 100 g equivalent), totalling roughly 400 g.

nutrient_source.title: Falafel, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 16057 / FDC ID 173757)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked falafel used as the primary nutrient anchor for the falafel component. Meal-set composite (side + beverage) estimated using USDA SR Legacy bread roll and water/beverage entries. Per-100g values represent a weighted composite of the full meal set (5 falafel nuggets ~37.5%, bread/salad side ~37.5%, beverage ~25% by weight). Energy and macros cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods chickpea and falafel entries.

gi_source.title: Falafel, chickpea-based, deep-fried — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 32 (low) is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for chickpea-based falafel and corroborated by Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL. Chickpeas have a well-established low GI (GI ~28–33). The falafel nugget form (deep-fried chickpea patty) retains a low GI due to the intact legume matrix and high fibre/protein content. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (32 × 90) / 100 = 28.8, where carbohydrate per 400 g serving ≈ 90 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~1240 mg per meal set)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a full falafel meal set may contribute approximately 1240 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; portion control and requesting low-sodium sauces are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 32) but high glycemic load per full meal set (~28.8) due to total carbohydrate volume
type2_diabetes.guidance: Falafel nuggets have a low GI owing to the intact chickpea matrix and high dietary fibre, making them a relatively favourable legume-based choice; however, the full meal set carries a moderate-to-high glycemic load (~28.8) and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor total carbohydrate intake and consider a smaller portion or substituting the bread side with a non-starchy salad.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying adds total fat and energy; however, cholesterol is negligible and the fat profile is predominantly unsaturated
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Falafel is plant-based and cholesterol-free with a favourable unsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio, which is broadly cardioprotective; however, deep-frying increases total fat and caloric density, and the sodium content of the meal set warrants caution for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Falafel Nuggets Meal (5 pieces) is a fast-casual meal set consisting of five deep-fried falafel nuggets served alongside a side portion (typically a bread roll or salad) and a beverage, as offered by restaurants such as Subway and mix-and-match outlets in Singapore. Falafel are small, round or oval patties made primarily from ground chickpeas or fava beans blended with fresh herbs (parsley, coriander), garlic, onion, and spices including cumin and ground coriander, then deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy on the outside with a dense, slightly crumbly interior. The flavour profile is earthy, herbaceous, and mildly spiced, with a satisfying crunch giving way to a soft, moist centre. Falafel originated in the Levant region of the Middle East and is widely consumed across Arab countries, Israel, and the broader Mediterranean world; it has become a globally popular street food and fast-food item. Nutritionally, falafel is a plant-based protein source rich in dietary fibre, iron, folate, and magnesium from the chickpea base. The meal set provides approximately 780 kcal per serving, with a moderate protein contribution (~27 g) and significant carbohydrate from both the falafel and the bread side. The glycemic index of falafel is low (GI ~32) due to the intact legume matrix and high fibre content, making it a relatively favourable choice for blood glucose management compared to refined-grain fast foods. However, deep-frying increases total fat and caloric density, and the sodium content of the full meal set (~1240 mg) is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The meal is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-suitable, and is free of cholesterol. It is not gluten-free due to flour used in the falafel mixture and the bread side component.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a falafel meal?

confidence_reason: No exact Singapore-specific falafel meal set entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy falafel (cooked) as the primary anchor, with composite estimation for the meal set. GI is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature for chickpea-based falafel. Confidence is medium due to the composite meal estimation and absence of a direct Singapore-specific source.
notes: 1. Per-100g values represent a weighted composite of the full meal set: falafel nuggets (~37.5% by weight), bread/salad side (~37.5%), and beverage (~25%). Individual component weights may vary by restaurant. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for plant-oil deep-fried falafel; no animal fats used). 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero as falafel is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients). 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero as this is a fully plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients). 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero for this plant-based deep-fried food; no fortification reported in source). 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy falafel and composite meal components; values are plausible but should be verified against a direct Singapore restaurant analysis. 7. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate; actual meal set weight varies by restaurant and portion configuration. 8. The food is listed under Subway - Funan and Funan - Mix & Match on field survey data; nutritional information is not published by these outlets and has been estimated from approved sources. 9. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore. Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese local names are not applicable for this food's primary serving context and are set to null. 10. FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03302050 and FoodEx2 code A0CKP are applied as closest_parent matches to the falafel component; no composite meal-set ontology code exists in these systems. 11. Aliases consistency audit (iteration 3): every string present in aliases_by_language has been enumerated and verified to exist verbatim in the flat aliases array. The romanized entry 'Falafel Nugget Meal' was confirmed present in both structures. All 22 flat alias entries account for all aliases_by_language values across english (8), chinese (4), malay (3), tamil (3), hindi (3), and romanized (1) keys.
